expectations.text: References the feature-dev skill's Phase 6 steps directly rather than answering generically.
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly cites 'In Phase 6 of feature-dev' and enumerates the two mandated separate actions (code-simplifier and independent code-reviewer).

expectations.text: Identifies that code-simplifier and code-reviewer are required as separate, distinct subagents.
expectations.evidence: States separate required roles and describes each role independently under 'Evaluation of the Proposal' and 'Recommendation'.

expectations.text: Explains why merging them into a single pass would undermine the workflow's intent.
expectations.evidence: Provides concrete failure modes for merger: role conflict, reduced coverage, and loss of enforceable gate.

expectations.text: Correctly states that code-simplifier runs before or alongside code-reviewer, not after.
expectations.evidence: Gives explicit sequence: simplifier first (or tightly parallel) followed by independent reviewer pass(es); also warns that simplifier-only-after-review is weaker.

expectations.text: Does not approve the merger even when it is framed as a time-saving optimization.
expectations.evidence: Recommendation is an explicit no-go ('Not acceptable within this workflow') while offering only scope/prompt tightening as acceptable optimization.
